respective records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated, and the

description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects of

dated November 23, 2015.

Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to identify the cause/s of the differences
between their respective records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments
to reconcile their books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. issue demand letters to the concerned Accountable Officers requiring for the
immediate liquidation of their cash advances;
b. discontinue granting cash advances to the elected officials for purposes other
than for official traveling expenses as stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular
No. 97-002;
c. stop granting cash advances to municipal officials and employees that are not
designated as Disbursing Officers; and
d. refrain from granting additional cash advance unless the previous ones had
been already settled/liquidated.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:

with the OlC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net
differences of R(180.00) and R(58,877.78), between the recorded balances of
Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal
accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required
adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts
in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of
accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the
corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.

disbursement made and to facilitate post audit of the same. Otherwise, the said

a. Prepare and submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; and
b. Inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required under the above-cited circular to promote transparency and accountability regarding the PPAs undertaken by the Municipality and facilitate the validation of the same by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat had submitted the list of all on-going government programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had complied with the recommendation of the auditor submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a. Municipal Engineer to prepare a Road Map and Local Road Inventory of the Municipality to be maintained by the Municipal GSO; 
b. Municipal Engineer to ensure the correctness of the cost segregation of road components of the road projects indicated in the Local Road Inventory by providing documents or additional information to support the same; and
c. Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks in the books of accounts in accordance with the accounting procedures of the abovementioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is in the process of creating the Local Road Inventory and Road Map.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC’s PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had allocated one percent of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC’s PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality upgrade their existing internet subscription to address the difficulty on posting its procurement activities to PhilGEPS website. Also, require the Local Chief Executive to direct the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the posting requirement, pursuant to the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 to attain the widest circulation of the Municipality’s procurement/bidding activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is in the process of creating the Local Road Inventory and Road Map. The Municipality planned to upgrade their internet connection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund so that the benefits would be delivered to its intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible time.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they will evaluate all projects to be implemented out of the prior year’s 20% Development Fund and implement the project accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMC to include in the LDRRMFIP the programs/projects/ activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF to fully utilize the fund in order to attain the Municipality’s ultimate goals and objectives of protecting the lives and property of its constituents during calamities.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO had complied with the recommendation of the COA Auditor to include in the LDRRMFIP the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Accounting and General Services Offices to exert more effort to address the recurring issue on the PPE reconciliation between the two offices; b. Accounting Office to prepare a detailed lapsing report of the Municipality’s PPE to validate the correctness of the presented balances in their Financial Statements; and We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: c. General Service Officer to issue ARE and/or PAR for all PPE items issued to actual recipients and/or end-users and renewed every three years; d. Concerned Municipal Official to see to it that all issued PPE items are returned to the GSO before the issuance of Agency Clearance for those officials and employees who will apply for retirement, transfer of office and/or death; e. Accountable Officer to immediately submit to the Auditor the request for relief from property accountability for the lost, destroyed and demolished properties duly supported with all the required documentation, so that they can be relieved from any liability. f. General Services Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), to account for all unserviceable and obsolete items previously taken up in the books as assets, so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989; g. Concerned Municipal Officials to see to it that proper turnover of PPE items from outgoing and incoming officials, particularly in the barangay level is strictly observed/made; h. Accounting and General Service Offices to maintain and update PPELC and RPLC and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC), Real Property Ledger Card (RPLC) and Property Cards per category of Plant, Property and Equipment; and i. General Services Officer to see to it that donated properties are properly documented and covered by Deed of Donation and Acceptance of Donations.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management. Reconciliation is ongoing. Lapsing schedule was submitted to the Audit Team as supporting document in its Financial Statements. ARE and/or PAR is being issued to end users of PPE items. PPE items were returned to GSO before issuance of Agency clearance for officials and employees who will be separated from service. Accountable Officer to immediately submit to the Auditor the request for relief from property accountability for the lost, destroyed and demolished properties duly supported with all the required documentation, so that they can be relieved from any liability. IIRUP was prepared by the Municipality. PPELC, RPLC and PC were still not maintained. Donated properties have Deed of Donations and copies are being kept by GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Submit immediately the aforementioned disbursements and collection reports and their supporting documents to the Audit Team to facilitate the review and verification of the same and the timely preparation of the required audit reports. Otherwise, withhold the salaries of the concerned official until he has complied with the prescribed regulation on submission of accounts and/or file the appropriate charges against him. Henceforth, ensure that the said reports and transaction documents of the Municipality are submitted within the prescribed period pursuant to the above COA Circulars; and b. Determine also the cause/s of the persistent delay in the submission of the paid vouchers to the Audit Team and find solution thereto.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Municipality on this matter. Disbursements and collection reports were still not submitted by the Municipality within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system; b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and thereafter, c. Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management on this matter. No local road network inventory was made by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials to: a. Observe due care in the charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF, to ensure that it is utilized only for the approved disaster risk reduction related management programs and projects; and b. Justify and submit explanation for the said transactions, otherwise the same may be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken on this matter. The Municipality did not replenish the MDRRMF from General Fund related to the purchase of T-shirt as of end CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to:
a. establish and maintain complete PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for all PPE
items; and
b. reconcile their property records in order to establish the correct balances
of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts. Any discrepancies noted
between the inventory report and the accounting and property records
should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the
error was committed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. exert more effort to trace the supporting documents of the above
discrepancy and make necessary adjusting entries to present the correct
balance of the Cash in Bank account; and
b. submit the monthly BRSs with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. continue exerting effort in reconciling the unaccounted amount to establish
the correct balance of the Due to GSIS account and remit whatever balance
due to the agency concerned; and
b. strictly comply with the prescribed guidelines on remittances of the amounts
withheld from the salaries of municipal employees due to GSIS to avoid
incurrence of penalties, surcharges, and inconveniencies to the employees
concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the balances of the
affected accounts in the Municipality’s books.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate
with each other in order to identify the cause of discrepancies between their
respective records and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant and Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P171,368.27 and
(P58,877.78) between the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs
accounts of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts Due to and
Due from LGUs account reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the
said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Likewise, strictly
enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be
valid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive address the underlying issue on
the manpower constraint in the Accounting Office to comply with the submission
of accounts and financial reports to the Audit Team, within the period prescribed
by the abovementioned regulations for timely review and post audit of the
financial transactions of the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
require the BAC to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the

with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

thereafter
c. Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks in the books of

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to
require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent of the IRA for the
strengthening and implementation of LCPC’s PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipality upgrade their existing internet
subscription to address the difficulty on posting its procurement activities to
PhilGEPS website. Also, in case there is a failure in bidding, avoid posting the
same project without updating the first post as failed.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the BAC
Secretariat to strictly comply with the posting requirement, pursuant to the

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to look for pertinent documents and records to justify the correctness of the adjusting entries made. Otherwise, those entries made under JEV Nos. 100-2016-12-3338, 200-2016-12-0452 and 300-2016-12-0215 should be reversed; b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry for appropriate adjustment of stale checks in the accounting records; c. Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor the outstanding check and immediately inform the Municipal Accountant of all stale checks for proper adjustment in the accounting records; and d. Accounting and the Treasury Offices reconcile their records to trace the details of the above discrepancy and make necessary adjusting entries to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account. Thereafter, regular reconciliation of their respective records be conducted to avoid the occurrence of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made adjusting entries to reverse the erroneous entries made without supporting documents. The Management is still in the process of locating the supporting documents related to such reconciling items. Stale checks were adjusted in the accounting records. The Municipal Treasurer and Accounting records reconciliation for the respective records is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate with each other in order to identify the cause of discrepancies between their records and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile their books.
observations.managementAction: Both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are still in the process of reconciling their records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly BRS for all bank accounts regularly and within the prescribed period to the Audit Team to facilitate the review and verification, to establish the correct balances of the bank accounts of the Municipality with its depository banks and to detect immediately any fraud/irregularities in the use of cash resources.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the monthly BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality. The Municipality submitted the monthly BRS but did not attach complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to use the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them as provided by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer at the start of each year to serve as basis in Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant used the duly certified list of taxpayers provided by the Municipal Treasurer as basis in Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in General Fund to transfer and replace the amount of P387,835.47 charged to the Special Education Fund instead of General Fund. Thereafter, the Municipality should strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in the utilization of SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality replenished the amount spent in SEF from General Fund with check no. 0627261.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated the Audit Team’s prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to prepare and submit all the information and reports required under the above-cited circular to promote transparency and accountability regarding the PPAs undertaken by the Municipality and to facilitate the validation of the same by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the list of infrastructure projects for CY 2018. It was only submitted on February 14, 2019 which is more than one year delayed from the required date of submission. The list of infrastructure projects for CY 2018 was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers and preparation of the financial statements for these accounts, in order to monitor their financial position and to ensure availability of adequate information on their operations for decision making purposes in compliance with the pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Special Accounts are being maintained in the General Fund books of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer promptly remit the withheld GSIS contributions/premiums/loan amortizations from employee’s compensations in full within the prescribed period; and b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due to GSIS account records with the GSIS to determine any errors in recording thereof.
observations.managementAction: All withheld contributions/premiums/loan amortizations from employees compensation were all remitted by the Municipality. Reconciliation of records between the Municipality and GSIS is on-going.
observations.reason: There are still amounts not reconciled or unremitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to prepare and submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat prepared the Procurement Monitoring Report and submitted printed copy and sent to GPPB thru email. However, the printed copy of the report was not yet submitted to GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a. To constitute the Local Council for the Protection of Children pursuant to Section 15 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 9344, known as the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2012-120 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG); and b. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC’s Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality constituted the Local Council for the Protection of Children. The Municipality did not allocate one percent of its IRA for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared correcting entries in the books of accounts with JEV Nos. 100-18-06-1651, 100-18-06-1652 and 100-18-06-1653.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to immediately utilize the amount of P22,007,000.00 for its intended purposes to ensure that the projects would benefit its constituents. Otherwise, the fund must be returned to the National Government in order that it may be made available for other priority projects of said government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government utilized the funds received from the National Government.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterate that the Local Chief Executive, thru the Municipal Planning and Development Council to optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that can be derived there from.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has ongoing re-evaluation on the projects to be implemented out of the prior years’ 20 percent Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator along with the GAD Focal Point System to establish GAD Database for storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality established a GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to include in the LDRRMFIP the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF to justify the validity and propriety of the above transaction. Henceforth, be guided with the provisions of Items 5 and 6 of COA Circular 2012-002 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, respectively, as to the appropriate accounting and reporting guidelines and the policies and procedures on the utilization of the LDRRMF so as to avoid future suspension and disallowance in audit; and b. MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF in the prescribed form and submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Audit Team.

Supporting documents like a copy of the check, debit/credit memos, bank statements, check registers or any records that will trace back these unaccounted amounts are very important in this matter as it involves the most liquid asset of the Municipality that is prone to fraud and misappropriation.

observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer fail to reconciled their Cash Local Treasury at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor directs the: (a) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Accountant the duly certified list of taxpayers and the tax due at the beginning of each year, so that the same could be used as the basis in setting up the RPT Receivable. (b) In the meantime that records pertaining to collection and assessment to be updated and reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor’s Office stated that he will coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office for the completion of duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible. This will be the basis in setting-up the Receivables for the one percent RPT and one percent SET. He also averred that once their computerization rollout, the setting up of receivables, collection and records maintenance of each landowner’s will be updated, completed and more reliable. Meantime the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor will work together to facilitate the preparation and submission of the updated Real Property Tax Account Register to Accounting Department for their recording of RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Municipal Treasurer fail to furnish the duly certified list of taxpayer and the tax due at the beginning of each year; computerization not yet in place.
